data.title: Flagstar Financial Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.14 misses by $0.01, revenue of $496M misses by $22.36M
data.description:
• Flagstar Financial press release (NYSE:FLG): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.14 misses by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $496M (-26.1% Y/Y) misses by $22.36M.
  • CET1 capital ratio improved to 12.33%, at or above peer group levels
  •   Book value per common share of $18.28
  •  Tangible book value per share of $17.24
  • Second quarter 2025 NIM was 1.81%, up 7 basis points compared to first quarter 2025, but down 17 basis points compared to second quarter 2024.

data.title: Carter's Non-GAAP EPS of $0.17 misses by $0.20, revenue of $585M beats by $17.95M
data.description:
• Carter's press release (NYSE:CRI): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.17 misses by $0.20.
  • Revenue of $585M (+3.7% Y/Y) beats by $17.95M.
  • Adjusted operating margin decreased to 2.0%, compared to 7.0% in the prior year period.
  • Net cash used in operations in the first half of fiscal 2025 was $8.3 million, compared to net cash provided by operations of $91.7 million in the first half of fiscal 2024. 

  • 2025 Business Outlook and Tariffs

    The Company continues to closely monitor the Administration’s proposed plans to implement significant new tariffs on products imported into the United States from a wide range of countries. If implemented, these additional tariffs would add substantially to the approximately $110 million in duties on imported product paid by the Company in fiscal 2024.

    The Company has estimated that the gross pre-tax earnings impact of additional import duties under an additional baseline tariff of 30% for China, 20% for Vietnam, 19% for Indonesia, and 10% for all other countries is expected to be approximately $125 million to $150 million on an annualized basis. Over time, the Company intends to offset these additional costs through a combination of changes to its product assortments, cost sharing with its vendor partners, changes to the mix of its production by country, and raising prices to end consumers and its wholesale customers. In the second half of fiscal year 2025, the Company anticipates a net additional baseline tariff impact to pre-tax earnings of approximately $35 million.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: INTC, PARA, and more
data.description:
Stock futures nudged higher on Friday morning, driven by strong performances from major technology companies and growing optimism over potential trade agreements ahead of the Trump administration’s August deadline.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Deckers Outdoor (NYSE:DECK) +11% — Shares surged after the company reported a strong FQ1, driven by double-digit sales growth in its HOKA and UGG brands, which offset a 19% decline in other segments and led to a 17% overall revenue increase, beating both company and Street expectations. CEO Stefano Caroti highlighted the solid start to FY26 and reiterated confidence in the brand portfolio despite ongoing global trade uncertainty. The rally was further fueled by upbeat FQ2 guidance, with sales projected between $1.38B and $1.42B (vs. $1.40B consensus) and EPS seen at $1.50–$1.55, with the midpoint above the consensus of $1.51.
  • Paramount Global (NASDAQ:PARA) (NASDAQ:PARAA) +3% — Shares climbed after the Federal Communications Commission approved the company’s acquisition by Skydance, removing the final regulatory hurdle in a lengthy takeover saga. The FCC granted license transfers following commitments by Skydance’s David Ellison to uphold fair, unbiased, and politically diverse news coverage at CBS. Notably, Skydance pledged to avoid DEI policies, appoint an ombudsman to monitor bias, and maintain a strong local news presence. FCC Chairman Brendan Carr said the move marks a needed shift amid declining public trust in national media.

Biggest stock losers

  • Intel (NASDAQ:INTC) -5% – Shares fell after the company posted mixed Q2 results and guided for Q3 adjusted EPS of $0.00, below the $0.04 consensus, despite revenue guidance of $12.6B–$13.6B (midpoint $13.1B) topping the $12.64B estimate. Under CEO Lip-Bu Tan, Intel is undergoing a major restructuring effort that includes cutting 15% of its global workforce to end the year with around 75,000 employees. The company is also scaling back or pausing major factory projects in Germany, Poland, and Costa Rica, with operations being consolidated in Vietnam and Malaysia instead.

data.title: Metals lead commodities in 2025, but industrial outlook turns cautious
data.description:
Metals have emerged as the top-performing commodity sub-asset class of 2025 so far, with precious metals up 26.2% and industrial metals rising 10.9% year-to-date as of July 22.

However, looking ahead to the rest of the year, BMI maintains a neutral to bearish outlook for industrial metals, citing expectations of weakening demand. 

This is primarily due to subdued consumption in China and a slower global growth outlook for 2025, with downside risks to the global economy remaining elevated.

"While further trade deals are likely to be announced before the August 1 deadline, reducing trade policy uncertainty, higher tariff rates would place additional pressure on the global economy," the brokerage said.

Growth in China, the world’s largest metals consumer, is likely to face mounting headwinds over the next few quarters from US tariff uncertainties and the waning impact from the consumer goods trade-in program that has boosted retail sales.

On a positive note, a weaker US dollar is likely to provide support for metal prices in the second half of the year, allowing metals to hold up given their inverse relationship with the greenback and keeping any losses contained, BMI analysts noted.

As for precious metals, gold prices are currently hovering around $3,346/oz (XAUUSD:CUR), having risen by 2.8% month-to-date as of July 22, boosted by safe-haven demand, a weaker US dollar, and improved investor appetite amid prospects of a more dovish Fed. 

The brokerage recently maintained 2025 gold price forecast at an annual average of 3,100/oz and remain neutral towards prices for the coming months.

It expects the US Fed's rate cut announcements later in 2025 and 2026 to hold the key to gold price increases going forward.

Additionally, BMI expects gold to continue benefiting from increased central bank purchases anduncertainty surrounding the Trump administration’s trade policy shifts, which it says, will place a floor under bullion.

data.title: Athabasca Oil reports Q2 results
data.description:
• Athabasca Oil press release (OTCPK:ATHOF): Q2 GAAP EPS of $0.11.
  • Average production of 39,088 boe/d (98% Liquids), representing 4% (15% per share) growth year-over-year.
  • The Company anticipates production at the upper end of guidance of 37,500 – 39,500 boe/d with an exit rate of ~41,000 boe/d.

data.title: Pony.ai launches 24/7 robotaxi service in Guangzhou, Shenzhen
data.description:
• Pony.ai (NASDAQ:PONY) has launched round-the-clock robotaxi operations in Guangzhou and Shenzhen, two major economic hubs in southern China.
  • This marks a significant milestone in the company's China expansion, extending service hours from 15 hours daily to full 24/7 availability.
  • In parallel, Pony.ai has also expanded its robotaxi testing window in Beijing to 24 hours a day.
  • PONY +2.21% premarket to $14.7.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: CenterPoint Energy to hire 200 new lineworkers in 2025 and nearly 800 by 2030
data.description:
CenterPoint Energy (NYSE:CNP) on Friday unveiled a series of efforts to achieve its goal of hiring 200 additional lineworkers by the end of this year, and nearly 800 by 2030.

CenterPoint's accelerated hiring efforts are expected to support energy investments over the next 10 years and help the Greater Houston area meet the rapidly growing demand for local electric workers.

As part of these efforts, CenterPoint is also hosting ongoing job fairs, including collaborating with regional workforce development networks.

These combined workforce expansion efforts will support CenterPoint's (NYSE:CNP) critical resiliency and infrastructure work, including over $53 billion in local energy investments as part of the company's 10-year capital expenditure plan.
